rulings.Case Title: INCENTIVE FINANCIAL SERVICES, LLC VS. BARRY K LIM
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Awarding Post Judgment Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Wednesday, July 11, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFF INCENTIVE FINANCIAL SERVICES, LLC Notice Of Motion For Order Awarding Post Judgment Attorney Fees. Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: This Motion was previously set for hearing on June 28, 2018, at which time the Judge Pro Tem ordered it continued to July 12 on the Court's own motion and ordered the moving party to immediately provide a courtesy copy of its motion filings with the Discover Department (Dept. 301). Moving party has failed to follow the Court's direction in this regard. Therefore, on the Court's own Motion, this matter is again continued, to July 26, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 302, and moving party shall immediatley provide a courtesy copy of its filings with the Discovery Department as previously ordered. If the moving party can show at the hearing currently set for July 12 that it in fact did previously provide courtesy copies of its papers to Dept. 301 as previously directed, then the Court is prepared to grant the motion; otherwise, the hearing shall be moved to July 26 as indicated above, pending moving party's immediate delivery of courtesy copies, with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date per SF Local Rule 2.7B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BABAK ABEDI MD VS. MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Writ Of Admin Mandate
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, July 12, 2018, Line 3, PETITIONER BABAK ABEDI'S Notice Of Hearing On Writ Of Admin Mandate. Petitioner Babak Abedi's petition for writ of mandate is denied. Per the independent judgment standard of review, the weight of the evidence supports the decision of respondent Medical Board of California to revoke Dr. Abedi's physician and surgeon's certificate. The record demonstrates that Dr. Abedi violated his probation on August 14, 2017 when he missed a biological fluid testing appointment. Dr. Abedi's claim that he was too sick to attend the appointment or contact his monitor lacks credence because he informed his work the same day that he could not appear. (RT I, 19:5-15; RT II, 42:13-17.) Dr. Abedi also violated his probation on August 18, 2017 when his diluted test sample tested positive for D-methamphetamine. Dr. Abedi speculates that the sample was tainted or that there was a break in the chain of custody. Dr. Abedi has not cited any competent evidence showing that the lab results or Dr. Kuntz's testimony regarding those results is unreliable. Based on these violations, the court cannot say that Board committed a manifest abuse of discretion by issuing an excessive penalty. The misstatement in the Board's decision that Dr. Abedi was convicted of driving under the influence in 2011 is harmless error. The record shows that the bases for the license revocation were the probation violations and not the 2011 incident. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Board is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LYNNE CRAWFORD VS. ROBERT J. LIS, AN INDIVIDUAL DOING BUSINESS AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, July 12, 2018, Line 17, DEFENDANT ROBERT J. LIS, (AN INDIVIDUAL DOING BUSINESS AS) Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Defendant Robert J. Lis' motion for determination of the good faith of his settlement with plaintiff Lynne Crawford is granted and the valuation of the assignment of Mr. Lis' cross-claims is reserved till trial. Two subcontractors filed "conditional oppositions" which did not oppose the granting of this motion provided that the valuation of the assignment of Mr. Lis' cross-claims against them is not determined now and is reserved till trial. Deferring valuation of all of Mr. Lis' cross-claims till trial is reasonable given the current uncertainty of the value of the assignment of those cross-claims. No other opposition was filed to this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Lis is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and includes standard language about the effect of granting a CCP 877.6 motion and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANNETTE ELLIOT VS. KIEN LAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant The Regents Of The University Of California'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, July 12, 2018, Line 13, Defendant The Regents Of The University Of California'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendant The Regents of the University of California's motion for summary adjudication on all causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Annette Elliott is granted as to all five causes of action and Ms. Elliott is granted leave to amend to file a second amended complaint only alleging a single cause of action for negligence based solely on the alleged negligent hiring, training and supervision of the Regents' employees subject to the conditions stated in the order on Ms. Elliott's motion for leave to file a second amended complaint. The Regents presented sufficient evidence to satisfy their burden of production on all five causes of action alleged in the first amended complaint and Ms. Elliott did not present any evidence creating a triable dispute on any of those claims. Based on the undisputed facts, the Regents are not vicariously liable for invasion of privacy, IIED, or negligence because defendant Kien Lam's misconduct was not within the scope of his employment nor was it ratified by the Regents. Videotaping people in a bathroom was not related to Mr. Lam's job duties and his misconduct was not foreseeable in light of his duties. Because the Regents took prompt and appropriate steps to investigate Mr. Lam's misconduct, put Mr. Lam on investigatory leave, and precluded Mr. Lam from returning to work until he resigned, the Regents' conduct shows that they did not ratify Mr. Lam's misconduct. The Regents are not directly liable for invasion of privacy per Civil Code 1708.8 because Mr. Lam confessed that he acted alone in installing the camera. The undisputed facts also show that the inoperable tampon dispenser did not constitute a dangerous condition of property. The Regents are not liable for sexual harassment per Government Code 12940 because Mr. Lam's misconduct did not alter the conditions of Ms. Elliott's employment to create an abusive working environment and the Regents did not ratify Mr. Lam's misconduct. There is no evidence that the Regents had any notice of sexual harassment conduct by Mr. Lam prior to learning of the camera in the bathroom. None of the evidence submitted by Ms. Elliott shows that the Regents had advance knowledge of Mr. Lam having placed a camera in the bathroom or that the Regents acted in any way to ratify Mr. Lam's misconduct. While evidence was presented that the Regents knew or should have known that Mr. Lam had engaged in peculiar, offensive and criminal behavior before the discovery of the camera in the bathroom, none of that evidence would put a reasonable person on notice that Mr. Lam was engaging in conduct that violated the privacy rights of any employee or constituted sexual harassment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Regents is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and also complies with CCP 437c(g) and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANNETTE ELLIOT VS. KIEN LAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Leave Of Court To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, July 12, 2018, Line 13, Plaintiff's Motion For Leave Of Court To File Second Amended Complaint. Plaintiff Annette Elliott's motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted in part subject to the following three conditions: a) Ms. Elliott may file a second amended complaint no later than July 23, 2018 only alleging a single cause of action for negligence based solely on the alleged negligent hiring, training and supervision of the Regents' employees; b) at the same time Ms. Elliott files her second amended complaint she must serve verified responses without objections to deem-served discovery to state all facts that support her sole remaining claim, identify all persons who know any of those facts and what facts they know, identify and produce all documents that refer or relate to those facts; and c) Ms. Elliott must agree that defendant The Regents of the University of California may file a summary judgment/adjudication motion directed to her sole remaining claim on 30 days rather than 75 days' notice. Permitting Ms. Elliott to allege a negligent hiring, training and supervision claim comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings at any time in a case. Conditioning that permission as set forth herein eliminates any possible legal prejudice to the Regents and allows for the resolution of this case without a further continuance of the trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Elliott is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and states that Ms. Elliott agrees to all three conditions and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ADRIENNE HOGAN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Judgment For Deft And Order Granting Deft'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, July 12, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF ADRIENNE HOGAN'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Judgment For Deft And Order Granting Deft's Motion For Summary Judgment. Plaintiff Adrienne Hogan's motion to set aside the judgment and order granting defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is granted. Ms. Hogan has shown that she is entitled to discretionary relief per CCP 437(b) because her mistaken belief that the November 2, 2017 order continuing the trial date also continued the date of her opposition to the City's motion for summary judgment was made in good faith and was reasonable in light of the relevant circumstances. Prior to substituting out of the case, Ms. Hogan's former counsel, Mr. Patten, informed Ms. Hogan that the stipulated motion seeking continuance would result in a new date for Ms. Hogan to respond to the motion for summary judgment. The November 21 order also stated that pretrial deadlines will be recalculated according to the new trial date. As a self-represented litigant, Ms. Hogan was reasonably diligent in bringing this motion, which was filed within six months of the entry of judgment on December 19, 2017. Ms. Hogan may file opposition papers to the City's motion for summary judgment no later than August 2, 2018. The City may file reply papers no later than August 15, 2018. The motion will be heard on August 24, 2018. Granting the motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and the City has not identified any legal prejudice it will suffer if Ms. Hogan is given another opportunity to oppose the City's motion for summary judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Ms. Hogan is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD GELBERD ET AL VS. SHINTA HALIM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel David Misrahi'S Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Thursday, July 12, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANTS SHINTA HALIM, ERICK KURNIAWAN Motion To Compel David Misrahi'S Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is unopposed and is therefore GRANTED. The proof of service indicates service on attorney Jonathan Bornstein (without specifying him as Mr. Misrahi's counsel), and the docket shows Mr. Misrahi's cross-complaint was filed on his behalf by attorney Jonathan Bornstein. Sanctions in the requested amount of $990.00 are awarded against both attorney Jonathan Bornstein and cross-complainant Misrahi, jointly and severally. Cross-complainant Misrahi is ordered to attend deposition by defendants/cross-defendants Halim and Kurniawan in accordance with their Re-Notice of Deposition and Demand for Production of Documents dated May 9, 2018, said deposition to take place within thirty days from the date of this Order or as the parties may otherwise agree in writing at least seven days before any such mutually agreed alternative date. If the deponent fails to appear as ordered herein, or as otherwise agreed in writing between the parties, then the Court shall entertain a further motion to compel and request for further sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MAUREEN D'AMBROSIO VS. DIGNITY HEALTH (A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION) ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, July 12, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANT PALO ALTO FOUNDATION MEDICAL GROUP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Palo Alto Foundation Medical Group's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is denied in its entirety and its ex parte application to continue the hearing on the motion is also denied. The declaration of Dr. Gavi shows that there is a triable dispute whether the conduct of the PAFMG's physicians fell below the standard of care in failing to conduct a neurological examination and order a cervical MRI before May 2015. PAFMG has not satisfied its summary motion burden to proffer admissible expert evidence on the issue of causation. (Sanchez v. Kern Emergency Medical Transportation Corporation (2017) 8 Cal.App.5th 146, 166 (To be admissible, an expert's opinion must not be purely conclusory and unaccompanied by a reasoned explanation connecting the factual predicates to the ultimate conclusion.)). Because PAFMG's motion is denied without consideration of the declaration of Dr. Lu, there is no purpose in continuing the hearing on the motion to enable PAFMG to depose Dr. Lu. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff Maureen D'Ambrosio is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MEGAN KOSNOSKI VS. NO MORE DIRT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant No More Dirt, Inc. Notice Of Motion To Compel Fairmont To Respond To Production Of Documents Set Two, Verification Privilege Log, Supplemental Response, And Monetary Sanctions. (For Tentative Ruling Purposes Only, Part 2 Of 2)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Thursday, July 12, 2018, Line 2, Cross Defendant No More Dirt, Inc. Notice Of Motion To Compel Fairmont To Respond To Production Of Documents Set Two, Verification Privilege Log, Supplemental Response, And Monetary Sanctions. (For Tentative Ruling Purposes Only, Part 2 Of 2) Fairmont is therefore hereby ordered to provide Further Supplemental Responses to RPD Nos. 1-18 within seven days from the date of this Order, said further supplemental responses to be verified. The Further Supplemental Responses shall include no objections, the objections having been overruled or withdrawn, as noted above. Each Further Supplemental Response shall clearly state that a diligent search - not a "reasonably" diligent search - and a reasonable inquiry has been made in an effort to comply with the demand, and whether or not responsive documents will be or have been produced, and, as to those RPD's to which the response is an inability to comply, the Further Supplemental Response shall state whether the inability to comply is because the particular category or item requested never existed, has been destroyed, has been lost, misplaced, or stolen, or has never been, or is no longer in the possession or control of Fairmont. If, despite its representations to the Court in its Opposition, Fairmont is nevertheless indeed withholding any documents pursuant to the attorney work product and attorney client communication privileges - or any privilege whatsoever -- then it shall provide a Privilege Log to that effect, also within seven days of this Order. Fairmont shall likewise produce any and all responsive documents within seven days from the date of this Order to the extent they have not previously done so. The Court finds that Fairmont's RPD Responses were not Code-compliant, and that cross-complainants failed to engage in good faith meet and confer, and that their opposition to the instant motion is not substantially justified; therefore, pursuant to C.C.P. Section 2031.310(h) the Court awards the moving party the requested monetary sanction of $4,064 against Fairmont and their attorneys jointly and severally. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (end of part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MEGAN KOSNOSKI VS. NO MORE DIRT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Fairmont To Respond To Production Of Documents Set Two, Verification Privilege Log, Supplemental Response, And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Thursday, July 12, 2018, Line 2, CROSS DEFENDANT NO MORE DIRT, INC.'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Fairmont To Respond To Production Of Documents Set Two, Verification Privilege Log, Supplemental Response, And Monetary Sanctions (Part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. The Motion to Compel Supplemental Responses and Documents is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Cross-complainant Fairmont, responding to the subject Requests for Production of Documents ("RPD's"), posed various objections that documents were equally available, that requests were harassing, that the definition of the term COMMUNICATION was overly broad and unduly burdensome, and/or that an RPD sought documents protected by the attorney work product and attorney client communication privileges. In their Opposition at page 5 lines 22-23, however, Fairmont states that their counsel has represented that "it is not withholding any documents based upon attorney-client privilege or work product," thereby indicating that the objections on those grounds were baseless, or that it has now waived those objections and is producing any such privileged documents. Contrary to Fairmont's statement at page 5 line 26 of its Opposition, it unequivocally asserted those privileges in response to particular RPD's. The Court overrules Fairmont's other objections as meritless, and finds by its statement in its Opposition that it has withdrawn its privilege objections. On the eve if its Opposition being due, Fairmont proffered unverified supplemental responses to RPD's 3-7, 11, 13, and 15, which nevertheless refer to withholding of privileged documents - in contrast to Fairmont's above-quoted statement in its Opposition. Fairmont has also failed to show that any purported Protective Order applies to the subject RPD's. (End of Part 1 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MEGAN KOSNOSKI VS. NO MORE DIRT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication As To The Cross-Complaint Against No More Dirt, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, July 12, 2018, Line 16, DEFENDANT FAIRMONT HOTEL MANAGEMENT, L.P.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication As To The Cross-Complaint Against No More Dirt, Inc. Cross-Complainant Fairmont Hotel Management, L.P.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. FHML is entitled to judgment in its favor and against cross-defendant No More Dirt, Inc, in the amount of $204,846.43. FHML has standing to sue for the amount it seeks per the defense and indemnity provisions in the 2012 Service Agreement which require NMD to defend and indemnify FHML for all attorneys' costs and fees incurred in the underlying action. Although the Service Agreement identified Fairmont Hotel Company San Francisco L.P. as the Owner and United Janitorial, Inc. as the Operator, the undisputed facts show that the parties intended the contract to cover the then-current owner of the Hotel, Mason Street Associates LLC, and its agent FHML, and the operator of the cleaning service, NMD. Although Fairmont Hotel Company San Francisco L.P. was dissolved at the time the Service Agreement was signed, Mason Street Associates LLC and No More Dirt performed under the contract. The expansive defense and indemnity language in the Service Agreement requires NMD to defend and indemnify the owner and its agents for "any and all claims," requires NMD to pay for attorneys' costs, and specifically includes any act or omission of an employee in both performance and non-performance of the employee's duties. This broad language covers the alleged sexual assault because Mr. Mendieta was working as an NMD employee although not performing his employment duties when he allegedly went to a guest floor and sexually assaulted plaintiff Megan Kosnoski. FHML has submitted unrebutted evidence that it has been agent of the Hotel owner since 1999. Whether or not FHML may have exceeded its contractual authority with regard to the underlying litigation does not deprive it of standing to sue for the amount it seeks nor does it create a triable issue whether FHML was an agent of the Hotel owner with respect to the underlying litigation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for FHML is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DON TUCKER ET AL VS. THE TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY OF CONNECTICUT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Don Tucker To Provide Amended Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Thursday, July 12, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT THE TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY OF CONNECTICUT'S Motion To Compel Don Tucker To Provide Amended Responses To Discovery. Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Traveler's Motion to Compel Amended Responses to Special Interrogatories Set One Nos. 1-8 is DENIED. Defendant Traveler's Separate Statement in Support fails to set forth as to each Special Interrogatory the reasons why the requested information is relevant and discoverable. Rather, Traveler's Separate Statement recites general principles relating to liberal discovery and assumes its entitlement to "a more complete picture of Tucker's work and provision of equipment at the 2901 Broadway project" (Traveler's Separate Statement, page 7 lines 3-4) without ever explaining why such information is relevant here. Rule of Court 3.1345 (c) (3). Without that showing, Traveler's motion fails. Traveler's Motion to Compel Documents Responsive to its RPD No. 36 is denied: it is overbroad to the extent it seeks documents which "relate" to Plaintiff's Responses to Special Interrogatories Set One. It also appears from Plaintiff's briefings here that Plaintiff has already produced any and all non-privileged responsive documents. Accordingly, Plaintiff is ordered to provide an Amended, verified, Response to RPD No. 36 preserving his objections and affirmatively stating under oath that any and all non-privileged responsive documents bearing on the information sought by Special Interrogatories Set One have already been produced. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES SPINGOLA VS. KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Cross-Complainant And Cross-Defendant Kaiser Foundation Health Plan, Inc. To Sever Cross-Claims From Personal Injury Action
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, July 12, 2018, Line 19, CROSS-DEFENDANT KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Cross-Complainant And Cross-Defendant Kaiser Foundation Health Plan, Inc. To Sever Cross-Claims From Personal Injury Action. Cross-complainant and cross-defendant Kaiser Foundation Health Plan, Inc.'s motion to sever the cross-complaints from the complaint is treated as a motion for bifurcation of the cross-complaints and, so treated, is granted. The cross-complaints will be tried separately from the trial on the claims alleged in the complaint and the trial date for the trial on the cross-complaints will be determined by the civil master calendar judge or the judge who presides over the trial of the complaint after the trial of the complaint is concluded. However, if the date of the trial of the complaint is continued by the master calendar judge to a day after January 1, 2019 the trial of the cross-complaints will cease to be bifurcated and the new trial date for the trial of the complaint will cover the trial of the cross-complaints as well. Requiring KFHP to participate in a trial in this calendar year is unfairly prejudicial to KFHP. A trial date on the cross-complaints in this calendar year would require KFHP to forego filing a summary motion asserting its contractual interpretation arguments or give KFHP insufficient time to prepare such a motion as well as prepare for trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for KFHP is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN M FRANKLIN VS. UNION AND POLK LP. A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Depositions Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Zephyr Real Estate And Sherri Howe
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Thursday, July 12, 2018, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS KEVIN FRANKLIN, KEEHAE PARK'S Motion To Quash Depositions Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Zephyr Real Estate And Sherri Howe. Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is DENIED because nothing in the moving papers or the docket indicates that service upon the parties by e-mail was agreed to in writing, or that this is an e-filing matter. If at the hearing of this matter the moving party can demonstrate that e-mail service was previously stipulated to - and, therefore, that defendants were properly put on notice of this pending motion -- then the Court would grant the motion based upon defendants' failure to oppose it, and would therefore order that the subject subpoena to Sherri Howe and Zephyr Real Estate, dated May 18, 2018 and signed by attorney Michael J. Betz, be quashed. rulings.Case Title: BRENDA MOORE ET AL VS. MEGAN ROHRER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Temporary Restraining Order.
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, July 12, 2018, Line 12, Order To Show Cause Re: Temporary Restraining Order. The motion for a preliminary injunction filed by plaintiffs in their capacity as board members of the Grace Infant Care Center is granted. Defendants, and all persons and entities working cooperatively with them, are enjoined from 1) withdrawing, transferring or using any funds in Wells Fargo Bank Account Nos. 6403387514 and 4155001278 for any purpose other than for the sole benefit of GICC per a request made by a board member of GICC; and 2) taking any action to sublease, license or occupy any space currently occupied by the GICC under the terms of the GICC license granted by the California Department of Social Services. Plaintiffs have shown that they are likely to prevail on the merits of their claims because the Church Constitution and Bylaws show that the GICC is a self-sustaining, independent organization that has been conducting its own business and finances for years. Plaintiffs have also shown that the June 2018 resolution adopted by the Church likely violates the Church Constitution, which clearly states that Church resolutions may not conflict with the Constitution and Bylaws. The Constitution also provides for a required procedure to change the Constitution and Bylaws, which was not followed by the Church. Plaintiffs have also demonstrated that they, the staff members of the GICC, the children participating in the GICC and their parents and guardians will suffer irreparable harm for which monetary relief will not be sufficient if the preliminary injunction is not issued. Because defendants disclaim any intent or desire to do anything that this injunction precludes them from doing, defendants will not suffer any harm by the issuance of the injunction. Plaintiffs must post a $10,000 bond no later than July 20, 2018 unless defendants agree in writing to waive the bond. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
